message:
fix(tui,cli): project-qualified agent identity, theme cache correctness, and dispatch hardening

Three related improvements across the TUI and CLI entry point:

**agents screen (tui_screens/agents.rs)**
- Introduce `AgentIdentity { project, name }` struct so per-agent caches
  (message counts, model names, status fades, flash ticks, stagger
  reveals, seen-agents set) are keyed by project+name instead of bare
  name.  This prevents cross-project collisions when agents in different
  projects share the same name.
- Replace `recover_agent_message_counts_from_sqlite()` with
  `recover_agent_rows_from_sqlite()` which fetches full rows (project,
  name, program, model, last_active_ts, message_count) via a richer SQL
  query with ORDER BY and LIMIT.  When the recovered row set is at least
  as large as the poller snapshot, it is used directly so that project
  metadata survives restarts.
- Add `SQLITE_AGENT_RECOVERY_LIMIT` constant (500) mirroring the poller
  cap.
- Show `[project]` suffix on duplicate agent names in the table view;
  add "Project" line to the detail panel.
- Update all HashMap/HashSet key types and event handlers
  (MessageSent, MessageReceived, AgentRegistered) to use AgentIdentity.
- Refactor tests to use `test_agent_row()` and `agent_identity()` helpers.

**theme palette cache (tui_theme.rs)**
- Extend `TuiThemePalette::current()` cache key from a single generation
  counter to a composite of generation, named-theme index, and ftui
  ThemeId.  This ensures `ScopedThemeLock` (used in tests) and any
  external callers that change the theme without going through our
  mutation functions still invalidate the cached palette.

**CLI entry point (main.rs)**
- Replace the two-step `am_interface_mode_from_env()` +
  `should_dispatch_to_cli_surface()` logic with a single
  `resolve_early_dispatch()` function returning an `EarlyDispatch` enum
  (`Mcp` or `Cli { invocation_name, deny_command }`).  This consolidates
  invocation-name detection, mode parsing, and serve-denial into one
  testable unit.
- Remove `render_cli_mode_missing_command()` — the CLI surface now
  handles missing subcommands internally via clap.
- Change `load_env_file_value()` to return `std::io::Result<Option<String>>`
  and `resolve_http_bearer_token_for_serve()` to return `Result<Option<String>, String>`
  so that a missing or unreadable `--env-file` produces a clear error
  message and exit code 2 instead of silently falling back to no token.
- Expand unit tests: five new tests for `resolve_early_dispatch()` cover
  invocation-name priority, zero-arg CLI routing, serve denial, default
  MCP mode, and invalid mode rejection.  Existing env-file tests updated
  for the new Result signatures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
